rxn_class_summary: Cobalt-catalyzed enantioselective C–H functionalization / spiroannulation of 8-aminoquinoline benzamides with maleimides, with a chiral phosphoric acid (CPA) co-catalyst controlling enantioselectivity

key_intermediates.name: Co(II)–pivalate–amide complex (benzamide bound to Co with pivalate ligand)
Bis(amidate) Co(II) complex (two benzamide ligands on Co)
Spirocyclic succinimide–isoindolinone product 3 bound to quinoline (released product)
Co(IV)–maleimide insertion intermediate ligated by CPA phosphate (m/z 839.1210)

electron_flow: Co(II) coordinates the 8-aminoquinoline amide and undergoes pivalate-assisted concerted metalation–deprotonation (CMD) of the ortho C–H bond; oxidation to Co(III)/Co(IV) and migratory insertion into the maleimide C=C, followed by C–N bond-forming reductive cyclization, delivers the spirocyclic product. The chiral phosphoric acid binds to the Co center and enforces enantioselectivity in the C–C/C–N forming step.
rate_determining_step: Product-forming step after C–H metalation (likely migratory insertion / C–N reductive elimination); C–H cleavage is not rate-limiting since KIE ≈ 1.0–1.2

probe_reagents.name: [D]5-1 (pentadeuterated benzamide substrate) – parallel KIE with CPA1
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 1.2 from parallel reactions with CPA1, indicating C–H cleavage is not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: [D]5-1 – parallel KIE without CPA1
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 1.0 without CPA1, confirming C–H activation is not rate-limiting in either regime

probe_reagents.name: HRMS aliquot analysis during catalysis
probe_reagents.observation: Detected masses consistent with Co(II)–amide–pivalate (m/z 408.0873), bis(amidate)Co (554.1142), Co(III)–insertion/cyclometalated species (739.1608), CPA-bound Co complexes (695.1004, 756.1447, 902.1693) and a Co(IV) maleimide-insertion species (839.1210)

probe_reagents.name: DFT computation of product racemization barrier
probe_reagents.observation: ΔG‡ = 48.3 kcal/mol for 36-(S) → 36-(R) rotation, t1/2 ≈ 3.2×10^18 yr, confirming configurational stability of the atropisomeric product

notes: HRMS detects multiple plausible on-cycle species, including CPA-ligated Co complexes, supporting direct coordination of the chiral phosphate to cobalt rather than a purely outer-sphere Brønsted-acid role. Near-unity KIE (1.0 without CPA, 1.2 with CPA) rules out turnover-limiting C–H metalation; CPA likely modulates a later enantiodetermining migratory insertion or C–N bond-forming step. Product atropisomerism is configurationally locked (DFT).
